---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(glue)
library(purrr)

manual_voyage_fxn <- function(df, cte_name = "manual_voyage_data") {
  
  # 1. Format the data based on BigQuery types
  # Strings get quotes, Timestamps get TIMESTAMP('...'), NAs get NULL
  formatted_df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # trip_start / trip_end get the TIMESTAMP('...') wrapper
      across(any_of(c("trip_start", "trip_end")), 
             ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "NULL", paste0("TIMESTAMP('", as.character(.x), "')"))),
      
      # Numbers (year, confidence, etc.) - keep as plain text (no quotes)
      # exclude the timestamp columns we just handled
      across(where(is.numeric) & !any_of(c("trip_start", "trip_end")), 
             ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "NULL", as.character(.x))),
      
      # Booleans - lowercase true/false (no quotes)
      # across(where(is.logical), 
      #        ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "NULL", tolower(as.character(.x)))),
      
      # Everything else (Strings) - wrap in single quotes
      # Again, excluding the timestamp columns to avoid double-formatting
      across(where(is.character) & !any_of(c("trip_start", "trip_end")), 
             ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "NULL", paste0("'", .x, "'")))
    )

  # 2. Build the rows: (val1, val2, ...)
  rows <- apply(formatted_df, 1, function(x) paste0("(", paste(x, collapse = ", "), ")"))
  all_rows <- paste(rows, collapse = ",\n    ")
  # 3. Define the Schema for the STRUCT
  # This ensures BigQuery doesn't guess the types incorrectly
  col_names <- colnames(df)
  
  # Mapping R names to BQ Types for the STRUCT definition
  schema_definition <- map_chr(col_names, ~ {
    if (.x %in% c("trip_start", "trip_end")) {
      return(paste(.x, "TIMESTAMP"))
    }
    # if (.x %in% c("year")) {
    #       return(paste(.x, "INT64"))
    #     }    
    return(paste(.x, "STRING"))
  }) %>% paste(collapse = ", ")

  # 4. Wrap it in the CTE syntax
  glue::glue("
    {cte_name} AS (
      SELECT * FROM UNNEST([
        STRUCT<{schema_definition}>
        {all_rows}
      ])
    )")
}
```

```{r}
# Your manually entered missing data
# Cure
row1 <- data.frame(
  year = 2025,
  ssvid = "629009188",
  vessel_id = "ae51bd83f-f41a-8f60-712e-499e3e4ef2c1",
  vessel_iso3 = "GMB",
  class_confidence = "1",
  vessel_class = "Fishing vessel",
  gear_type = "",
  trip_id = "manual_add",
  trip_start = as.POSIXct("2025-12-12 22:55:05", tz = "UTC"),
  trip_end = as.POSIXct("2025-12-29 18:30:22", tz = "UTC"),
  trip_start_anchorage_id = "0ea1b22b",
  trip_end_anchorage_id = "0ec17255",
  trip_start_visit_id = "85bfdf501f5b7ee54f74a9767ccd7997",
  trip_end_visit_id = "6f590e2d2c359a2868ef7781608e7c61",
  trip_start_confidence = 2,
  trip_end_confidence = 3,
  start_label = "NOUADHIBOU",
  start_iso3 = "MRT",
  end_label = "DAKAR",
  end_iso3 = "SEN",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# LUQINGYUANYU199 
row2 <- data.frame(
  year = 2025,
  ssvid = "412371178",
  vessel_id = "4816d2eaa-a8ef-d96e-585b-5262e7324dac",
  vessel_iso3 = "CHN",
  class_confidence = "1",
  vessel_class = "Fishing vessel",
  gear_type = "drifting_longlines",
  trip_id = "manual_add",
  trip_start = as.POSIXct("2025-09-04 15:58:18", tz = "UTC"),
  trip_end = as.POSIXct("2026-01-31 13:59:59", tz = "UTC"),
  trip_start_anchorage_id = "0ec1741b",
  trip_end_anchorage_id = "0ea1b22b",
  trip_start_visit_id = "7d4d51bdc8d0021c6d64c49cece608f8",
  trip_end_visit_id = "7efd20a943ff6e51fae18ad6b29b49d8",
  trip_start_confidence = 4,
  trip_end_confidence = 3,
  start_label = "DAKAR",
  start_iso3 = "SEN",
  end_label = "DAKAR",
  end_iso3 = "SEN",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

missing_voyages_df <- bind_rows(row1, row2)

# Convert to SQL string
manual_voyage_cte <- manual_voyage_fxn(missing_voyages_df, cte_name = "manual_voyage_data")

# create a temp table for query 
temp_table_voyages <- '`world-fishing-827.scratch_joef.civ_q4_25_manual_voyages_temp`'

# load query
manual_voyages_q <- readr::read_file(here::here("queries","proactive", "manual_voyage_query.sql"))

# Execute via fishwatchr
manual_voyages <- 
fishwatchr::gfw_query(query = glue::glue(manual_voyages_q, 
                                         manual_voyage_cte = manual_voyage_cte, # manual voyage 
                                         start_date = start_date,
                                         end_date = end_date,
                                         year = year,
                                         start_year = start_year,
                                         end_year = end_year,
                                         temp_table = temp_table_manual,
                                         missing_ssvids = missing_ssvids,
                                         port_iso = country_iso3),
                                 run_query = TRUE,
                                 con = con)

voyages_with_coverage <- fishwatchr::gfw_query(
  query = glue::glue(ais_cov_q, 
                     manual_cte = manual_voyage_cte, # Your new parameter
                     temp_table = temp_table_voyages,
                     start_date = start_date,
                     end_date = end_date),
  run_query = TRUE,
  con = con
)$data
```


Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
